learning_outcomes.description: acquire French vocabulary and grammar appropriate to talking about yourself, your family and interests
interact with native and non-native speakers in basic French about your identity, daily life, interests and preferences
write and read sentences and simple texts about yourself, your family, your preferences, daily life
grow cultural awareness and openness to others in a global world
acquire strategies appropriate to learning a language

assessments.due_date:
7/08/2026

WEEK 2

Students who are not able to meet the due date (due to late enrolment) must contact the Course Coordinator as soon as possible to ensure they comply with this requirement.
assessments.task_description:
Duration: 5-10 minutes, depending on the student's background in French (no prior knowledge of French required).

It is necessary to be placed in the course prior to the end of Week 2 so that students can seek alternative courses if necessary. All students are required to demonstrate they have been correctly placed in FREN1010. Failure to do so will result in a maximum grade of 3 in the course. This is a hurdle requirement. No assessment items will be marked until this has been completed. Visit https://courseplacements.languages-cultures.uq.edu.au/

Completion of this Language Placement Questionnaire is a compliance hurdle requirement in many courses. This requirement aims to ensure that you are placed in the most appropriate language course based on your current level of linguistic proficiency. Your responses, along with the possible submission of short writing or speaking sample, help us match you to a course that aligns with your previous (formal or informal) language learning experiences.

Please note that this course includes a final hurdle assessment task where the use of generative AI or MT tools is not allowed. You will be expected to demonstrate a clear understanding of your own work without the assistance of AI or MT tools.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
For this activity, you will listen to and answer questions in French. The interaction will be based on grammar and vocabulary from Units 1 and 2 as well as in-class materials. 

REQUIREMENTS: You are required to have stable access a working microphone and camera to complete this activity. Technological problems are not a valid excuse for an extension.

This assessment item allows you to demonstrate competency at a low A1 level on the CEFR language proficiency scale for Spoken Interaction and Listening. (Link to Common European Frame of Reference)

This is an untimed video/audio submission. Other students will be able to view your submission. Detailed task information and criteria will be available on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
25/09/2026 5:00 pm

WEEK 9

This task will be open from 8am to 5pm on Inspera.
assessments.task_description:
In this task you will be required to demonstrate competence in writing using the grammatical structures and verb forms studied in weeks 1-8. You will write 120-180 words in response to a prompt. You will be penalised proportionately for not respecting the word limit.

Duration: 25 minutes

This will be an online, timed, writing task completed in Inspera. You are allowed to use your book, dictionaries and typeit.org but no automatic translation software or other online resources. You must have access to stable internet to complete this activity. ** If you require alternative exam adjustments, please apply for them to be considered for this assessment. **

This assessment item allows you to demonstrate competency at A1 level on the CEFR language proficiency scale for Writing. (Link to Common European Frame of Reference)

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
16/10/2026 5:00 pm

WEEK 11

This activity will be available from 8 am to 5 pm on Inspera.
assessments.task_description:
The listening activity will be based on Défi Actuel 1 Units 1-4 (Livre + Cahier) and all materials distributed in class or on Blackboard. Students will watch a video recording of a French speaker and answer questions about the content of the interview. 

Duration: 25 minutes + 5 minutes perusal time.

** If you require alternative exam adjustments, please apply for them to be considered for this assessment. **

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
9/11/2026 - 13/11/2026

WEEK 1 of EXAM BLOCK, date and time determined by sign up. The student's assigned timeslot will be considered the due date and time for this assessment.

The Oral Interview will be conducted via Zoom.
assessments.task_description:
The subject of the Oral Interview will be published in Week 11. In preparation, students will work in groups of two to practise topics, vocabulary and structures covered in Défi Actuel 1 Units 1-4. 

Duration: approx 4 mins per student (groups of two students are scheduled for 10 minutes)

The Oral Interview will be conducted via Zoom.

On the day of the assessment, students will participate in a simulation run by a staff member in French. They will ask and answer questions using expressions and structures studied in the course. Students are responsible for forming a group (selecting a partner and obtaining contact details) at least two weeks prior to the assessment. The preparation work is to be completed outside of class. Students are individually marked but demonstrate ability to interact in French by working with a group. If a team member requests an extension for whatever reason, the extension will only be valid for the team member and not the whole group. An official UQ ID card is required for attendance at the final oral interview.

This assessment item allows you to demonstrate competency at A1 level on the CEFR language proficiency scale for Spoken Production, Spoken Interaction and Listening. (Link to Common European Frame of Reference)
assessments.submission_guidelines: This Oral assessment will be recorded and the recording retained for 12 months on a secure UQ system in accordance with UQ policy specifically Assessment Procedure Section 6 - Recording and Reporting . Access to recordings will be limited to the relevant School, Faculty and Student & Academic Services staff and only accessed for the purpose of reviewing recordings.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
